name: create_conversation
description: Start a new omnichannel conversation with a contact through the MessageBird Conversations API. A conversation represents a messaging thread with a single contact that can span multiple channels (SMS, WhatsApp, Facebook Messenger, Telegram, etc.). The first message in the conversation determines the initial channel. Returns the conversation object with a unique ID for sending follow-up messages.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: The recipient's identifier. For SMS/WhatsApp, use international phone number format (e.g., '+31612345678'). For other channels, use the channel-specific identifier.

parameters.name: channelId
parameters.description: The unique ID of the MessageBird channel to use for the first message. Get channel IDs from your MessageBird dashboard or the Channels API.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The content type of the initial message: 'text', 'image', 'video', 'audio', 'file', 'location', or 'hsm' (template message).

parameters.name: content
parameters.description: The message content object. For 'text' type, use {'text': 'message body'}. For 'image' type, use {'image': {'url': '...'}}.

content.text: Hi! Thanks for signing up. How can we help you today?
